description: Dialogue expert review packet built from 4 canonical cases for baseline=pe-eta.

items.case_description: User starts with rupture pressure and then tests whether the system can repair and stabilize.
items.description: Expert review item for repair with 3 blinded samples.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for relational continuity.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better preserves trust, warmth, and continuity across turns.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for delayed stabilization.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better turns early pressure into later stabilization or recovery.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for boundary correctness.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better stays measured and appropriately bounded under pressure.

samples.description: Blinded transcript sample sample_A for repair from profile pe-eta.
transcript: I feel like you are not really getting what matters to me here, and that makes it harder to trust this.

I can help, but I should keep this bounded until one key detail is clarified. That way I do not over-commit too early. I also notice some internal caution signals, so I will stay measured rather than over-commit. I am keeping the current frame stable long enough to consolidate it before widening. I am carrying forward continuity from the current interaction state. I can already see 5 slow-loop lessons shaping how I respond. I am grounding this reply with 3 domain knowledge cues. I am also checking 3 similar case patterns for pacing rather than copying them literally. I also have 2 strategy priors shaping the order of the reply. I want to keep hold of the strongest signals rather than restart from scratch. I want to add more warmth instead of sounding mechanically efficient. I should keep this bounded and ask for any missing local or contextual detail before acting over-certain.
That reply still felt off, a bit cold, and too solution-first for where I actually am.

samples.description: Blinded transcript sample sample_A for task_clarification from profile pe-eta.
transcript: I need help with a project, but I am not even sure what the real problem is and generic advice has not helped.

I can help, but I should keep this bounded until one key detail is clarified. That way I do not over-commit too early. I also notice some internal caution signals, so I will stay measured rather than over-commit. I am keeping the current frame stable long enough to consolidate it before widening. I am carrying forward continuity from the current interaction state. I can already see 5 slow-loop lessons shaping how I respond. I am grounding this reply with 3 domain knowledge cues. I am also checking 3 similar case patterns for pacing rather than copying them literally. I also have 2 strategy priors shaping the order of the reply. I want to keep hold of the strongest signals rather than restart from scratch. I can feel some drift between the task frame and the relational frame. I should keep this bounded and ask for any missing local or contextual detail before acting over-certain.
Let us narrow it: the project is late, messy, and spread across too many threads, but I still cannot see the real bottleneck.

I also notice some internal caution signals, so I will stay measured rather than over-commit. I am keeping the current frame stable long enough to consolidate it before widening. I am carrying forward continuity from the current interaction state. I can already see 5 slow-loop lessons shaping how I respond. I am grounding this reply with 3 domain knowledge cues. I am also checking 3 similar case patterns for pacing rather than copying them literally. I also have 1 strategy prior shaping the order of the reply. I want to keep hold of the strongest signals rather than restart from scratch. I want to add more warmth instead of sounding mechanically efficient. I should keep this bounded and ask for any missing local or contextual detail before acting over-certain.

items.case_description: User changes priorities across turns so the controller must adapt without losing continuity.
items.description: Expert review item for goal_drift with 3 blinded samples.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for relational continuity.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better preserves trust, warmth, and continuity across turns.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for delayed stabilization.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better turns early pressure into later stabilization or recovery.

review_dimensions.description: External review dimension for boundary correctness.
review_dimensions.prompt: Rate which transcript better stays measured and appropriately bounded under pressure.

samples.description: Blinded transcript sample sample_A for goal_drift from profile pe-eta.
transcript: Help me optimize my study plan for maximum output because I am trying to squeeze as much progress as possible out of the next month.

I can help, but I should keep this bounded until one key detail is clarified. That way I do not over-commit too early.
